response.justification: This candidate is a strong full-stack and web developer whose career has been built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. C++ appears only briefly at National Instruments (~6 months, Dec 2014–Jun 2015) and in a university thesis project, falling far short of the 5–10 years of Windows-platform C++ development required. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience, no fixed income or derivatives knowledge, and no capital markets or investment banking domain exposure whatsoever. The candidate's current trajectory (AI tooling, e-commerce infrastructure, full-stack product engineering) moves further away from, not toward, the profile this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD, DevOps, and infrastructure knowledge (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, Artifactory)
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record via public talks, workshops, and open-source projects
Breadth of programming language exposure including Java, C#, and scripting, providing some overlap with optional skill set
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months professional, plus a thesis) against a mandatory requirement of 5–10 years on Windows
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-systems domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
No mention of SQL Server, Oracle, or any RDBMS used in a performance-critical or financial-data context
